subject.title: German B
subject.description:
**IB German B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: Identities, Experiences, Human ingenuity, Social organization, and Sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive language skills through written and spoken texts relating to German culture and society**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2018** for the current syllabus.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills (writing) | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills (listening + reading) | 1h 45min | 2h | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment based on visual stimulus | Teacher assessed | Teacher assessed | 25% |

strengths: Use of varied, topic-specific lexis and occasional idiomatic expressions (e.g. ânicht nur halbherzigâ, âfÃ¼hrt uns dazu, darÃ¼ber nachzudenkenâ)
Wide range of grammatical structures including coordinating and subordinating conjunctions, relative clauses and infinitive constructions
Generally clear and effective communication with only occasional errors that do not impede understanding
weaknesses: Occasional word-order and verb inversion mistakes (e.g. missing inversion after âalsoâ)
Literal calques in places (e.g. âNur, hat der Jungeâ¦â) that reduce naturalness
Some repetition of sentence openers and lexis (e.g. repeated use of âAlle Menschenâ)
Minor punctuation errors (missing commas before subordinate clauses)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place vocabulary and grammar solidly in the 7â9 band, accuracy at the higher end of 7, and inferred pronunciation in the 4â6 band. The studentâs selective use of idiomatic expressions and complex structures pushes performance into the upper half of the 7â9 band. In balancing occasional awkwardness against overall clarity and range, 9/12 is justified.

strengths: Presentation remains consistently relevant and persuasive in linking details of the extract to the theme
Concrete references to visual elements (Handschuhe, PlastiktÃ¼ten, Gruppen im Meer) anchor the observations
Effective use of the theme âDen Planeten teilenâ to unify the analysis
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally stays at a general level without deeper symbolic or critical exploration
Observations could be more tightly developed with textual evidence and discussion of metaphorical meaning
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevance to the extract is sustained and convincing, meeting the 5â6 band. Use of the extract yields competent and supported observations but falls short of fully developed critical analysis. Thus 5/6 aligns with âconsistently relevantâ and upper-end âcompetent useâ.

strengths: Responses are consistently relevant, appropriate and build on each question
Personal examples (street litter, reusable bags, tree planting) add depth and authenticity
Clear engagement with the topic, showing personal stance and broader reflections
weaknesses: Minor lexical hesitations interrupt fluency occasionally
Further nuanced personal anecdotes could deepen the discussion even more
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs answers meet the top-band descriptors for response relevance, appropriateness and depth, providing broad scope, personal interpretation and sustained development. Full 6/6 is warranted.

strengths: Sustained comprehension and clear multi-sentence responses to each prompt
Continuous participation in the target language without undue reliance on repetition
weaknesses: Limited evidence of independent contributions (no clarifying or follow-up questions)
Missed opportunities to steer or deepen the interaction proactively
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Interaction is maintained with clear comprehension, fitting the 3â4 band. However, lack of initiative and independent contributions prevents placement in the 5â6 band. A mid-band score of 4/6 accurately reflects this performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: A solid variety of structures is demonstrated: coordinating and subordinating conjunctions (weil, dass, wenn), modal constructions, relative clauses and infinitive clauses (âum â¦ zu schÃ¼tzenâ). Complex sentences are generally well formed, yet several inaccuracies (âsollten die Jungen dem Ort schon fleiÃig sauber gemacht habenâ; missing verb inversion after âalsoâ) reveal limits in control. The effective yet not fully selective use of complex grammar supports placement at the lower end of the 7-9 band.

Without an audio file, pronunciation and intonation can only be inferred from orthography. Given the generally correct spelling of German phonetic patterns and absence of obvious anglicised spellings, it is reasonable to assume clarity sufficient for comprehension, though enhancement of meaning cannot be verified. A mid-band judgement (4-6) is therefore most defensible.

The student provides complete answers and maintains participation, but evidence of independent contributions beyond the examinerâs prompts is limited in the transcript. The performance meets descriptors for âmostly sustained participationâ without clearly demonstrating pro-active discourse management, fitting a solid 3-4 band with 3 marks.

Every element of the presentation links directly to details in the visual stimulus: clothing, setting, weather, actions and broader theme âDen Planeten teilenâ. The commentary remains consistently on topic and persuasive in highlighting environmental responsibility, satisfying âconsistently relevant and convincingâ at 5-6 marks; minor digressions are minimal, so 5 marks are appropriate.

Responses are elaborated with personal examples (street litter, reusable bags, tree planting) and reflections on broader impact, showing both breadth and some depth. While interpretation could be more sophisticated, engagement with the topic is evident and personal stance is clear, aligning well with the 5-6 band.

In the follow-up conversation, each answer addresses the prompt directly and appropriately, expanding on recycling, personal actions and education. No question appears ignored or misunderstood, and relevance is consistently maintained; hence the descriptor for 5-6 marks is met, with a mid-band score of 5 reflecting minor lexical hesitations within responses.

The written transcript demonstrates sustained understanding of each question and continuous interaction through multi-sentence answers. However, because initiative (e.g. asking clarifying questions, steering discussion) cannot be confirmed from the text alone, full marks in the top band are not justified. Thus a high 3-4 band score is appropriate.

The student cites concrete elements (Handschuhe, PlastiktÃ¼ten, Gruppen im Meer) and extrapolates environmental implications, moving beyond mere description. Support is present but sometimes general (âzeigt, wie wichtig es ist unsere Umwelt zu schÃ¼tzenâ), and analytical depth could be further developed (e.g. no detailed discussion of symbolism). This meets âcompetent use â¦ some developed observationsâ, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The student employs a wide range of everyday and topic-specific lexis (z.B. âhalbherzigâ, âRessourcen schonenâ, âfreiwillig den Strand gesÃ¤ubertâ) that is consistently appropriate to the environmental theme. Word choice is seldom repetitive and occasional idiomatic turns of phrase (âdie kleinste Hilfe zÃ¤hlt auchâ, âfÃ¼hrt uns dazu, darÃ¼ber nachzudenkenâ) are present, though not pervasive. While a few instances of literal translation (âNur, hat der Jungeâ¦â) show minor awkwardness, overall variation and suitability meet the descriptor for the 7-9 band.

Communication remains clear throughout; errors (article-noun agreement, preposition choice, word order) are noticeable but rarely impede meaning. Basic structures are largely accurate, and lapses in complex clauses cause only slight hesitation for the listener. This overall accuracy with occasional interference fits the descriptor for âmostly accurate with occasional errors that donât interfereâ, warranting 7 marks.

annotations.comment: The phrase âAlso deswegen die Jungen im Vordergrund auch Shorts tragenâ is redundant. Choose one discourse marker (âAlsoâ or âdeswegenâ) to tighten the phrasing.
The image serves as a strong anchor for your analysis, immediately contextualizing the environmental theme and supporting your later observations.
The description of the three boys in red T-shirts is detailed and uses varied lexis to convey age, roles, and equipment effectively.

The clause âNur, hat der Junge auf der rechten Seite Handschuhe und Schuhe getragenâ reads as a literal calque. Consider replacing âNurâ with âJedochâ or âAllerdingsâ for a more natural flow.
Your idiomatic phrase ânicht nur halbherzigâ enhances the communicative impact and demonstrates varied vocabulary.
The transition âalso nicht nur halbherzigâ lacks inversion after âalso.â Reorder to âalso nicht nur halbherzig vorgehen,â ensuring verb-second word order.
Revise âsollten die Jungen dem Ort schon fleiÃig sauber gemacht habenâ to correct word order and tense, e.g. âhaben den Ort bereits fleiÃig sauber gemacht.â
Consider combining your background observation sentences into one cohesive statement to improve flow and coherence.
Your consistent link to the theme âDen Planeten teilenâ in the background paragraph convincingly anchors your analysis.
To deepen your analysis, explore the symbolic significance of the swimmers in the waterâwhat might they represent in relation to shared responsibility?
The closing call âdie kleinste Hilfe zÃ¤hlt auchâ effectively reinforces the collaborative message and invites reflection.
Vary your sentence openers in the conclusion to avoid starting repeatedly with âAlle Menschenâ. Consider âUnsere globale Gemeinschaftâ¦â for stylistic impact.
How might you further develop the symbolic interpretation of the background swimmers to enhance analytical depth?
Your personal framing of environmental protection as âunser Zuhauseâ provides a clear, relatable rationale in the first response.
Repetition of âunsereâ in consecutive clauses weakens variety. Consider synonyms like âdiese,â âjene,â or restructuring to avoid redundancy.
Replace âbewahrenâ with âerhaltenâ or âschÃ¼tzenâ for a more precise verb choice in conservation contexts.
Your personal examplesâpicking up street litter, beach clean-upsâadd authenticity and depth to your response.
To demonstrate stronger interactive skills, incorporate a clarifying question in your response to engage the examiner.
In âunsern MÃ¼ll recyceln und trennenâ the infinitive requires âzu recycelnâ; adjust to âunseren MÃ¼ll zu recyceln und zu trennen.â
Avoid using both âauÃerdemâ and âkÃ¶nnen wir auchâ in the same clause; choose one to streamline the sentence.
In âleg ich persÃ¶nlich weg,â the verb should be in second position: âlege ich persÃ¶nlich weg.â
The term âwiederverwertbare TÃ¼tenâ is misleading; use âwiederverwendbare TÃ¼tenâ to refer to reusable bags.
You effectively highlight the role of parents and schools in raising environmental awareness, demonstrating engagement with the prompt.
A comma is needed before the subordinate clause: âlernen, wie wichtig die Umwelt istâ to clarify sentence structure.
Include more personal anecdotes or reflections in your follow-up answers to deepen response development and bring unique perspective.
How might you integrate a probing follow-up question to boost interaction and show sustained engagement?

similarExemplars.title: Wie haben die ost- und westdeutschen Medien ideologische Unterschiede durch Wortwahl und Wortfrequenz gespiegelt?
